signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Rate Steady at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Rate Steady at 2.5% for 7th Consecutive Meeting | NO | HIGH confidence**

The Bank of Korea announced its seventh consecutive interest rate freeze at 2.5% in early April 2026, with no rate cuts anticipated despite moderating inflation[3]. South Korea's annual inflation rose to **2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the prior two months but below the 2.4% forecast[1][3].
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Rate cuts remain off the table due to these dual pressures[3]
signals.raw: Rate cuts remain off the table due to these dual pressures[3]

signals.headline: Market implications:**
signals.raw:
Market implications:**
The central bank's decision signals an extended pause in monetary easing, contrary to earlier expectations of potential rate cuts when inflation dipped to 2.0% in January-February[2]. Policymakers remain focused on property-price stabilization and currency volatility rather than inflation-driven rate cuts[2].
